documents.name: zorblax-protocol.md
documents.content: The Zorblax Protocol was ratified in 1987 by the Council of Mirelle. It established three tiers of access: bronze, silver, and gold. Gold-tier members may convene an emergency session with a two-thirds vote.

documents.name: mirelle-city.md
documents.content: The city of Mirelle sits on the banks of the Veldt River. Its population is 240,000. Mirelle was founded in 1402 and is famous for its glass bridges.

documents.name: council-procedures.md
documents.content: Council sessions are held quarterly. The quorum for a session is 12 members. Votes are recorded by the Scribe of Records. Bronze-tier members may observe sessions but may not vote.

documents.name: veldt-river.md
documents.content: The Veldt River flows 800 kilometres from the Aether Mountains to the Saline Sea. Its annual flood deposits the fine silt that makes Mirelle's glassmaking possible.

documents.name: glass-guild.md
documents.content: The Mirelle Glass Guild was founded in 1455. Its master artisans are called Luminors. The guild's signature product is the Veldt prism.

documents.name: aether-mountains.md
documents.content: The Aether Mountains are the source of the Veldt River. Their highest peak is Mount Caldera at 4,200 metres. The range is rich in cobalt ore.

documents.name: saline-sea.md
documents.content: The Saline Sea is landlocked, with salinity three times that of the ocean. Only the Drift Eel survives in its waters. Fishing there has been banned since 1990.

documents.name: silver-tier.md
documents.content: Silver-tier members may propose motions, but each motion needs a sponsor from the gold tier. Silver tier is granted after five years of service.

documents.name: mirelle-festival.md
documents.content: The Lumen Festival is held every autumn equinox. It features floating lanterns set adrift on the Veldt River. The festival began in 1502.

documents.name: scribe-records.md
documents.content: The Scribe of Records is appointed for life and maintains the Codex. The current Scribe of Records is Elen Vos.
